title: lonchocarpus longipedunculatus
intro: Lonchocarpus longipedunculatus, also known as the Mexican walnut or Guanacaste, is a tree species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The seeds of Lonchocarpus longipedunculatus have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and are believed to offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage their medicinal properties.
